Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Hi Alexey, it's unclear to me use of ${name} is inconsistent
- why for server packages only?
- why for some, but not all, Requires even within server?
Thanks

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

because ${name} addresses an Name: spec line.

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Hopefully this makes my question more clear.

Patch Set 4:

in some cases - "lustre" is real package name, in some cases "lustre" is virtual name for dependences. Example like - I can deploy an OSD outside of lustre tree and choose a different _package_ name - but as it provide an "lustre-osd" and "lustre-client" object - dencedes will be resolved. It have no requirements to be lustre_name-client or lustre_name-osd.

Patch Set 4:

if you mean a lustre-iokit - it's traditional name and don't have dependences to the different modules or kernel parts - so it don't need to be changed to different name.

Olaf, can you reply ?

parents.subject: LU-12780 osp: don't use ptlrpc_thread for opd_update_thread

commit.subject: LU-12214 build: fixes if the name is not just 'lustre'
commit.message:
LU-12214 build: fixes if the name is not just 'lustre'

fix using a %{name} macro in spec file.
this allow to have right names for server packages.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-5915
Test-parameters: trivial
Change-Id: I2ae271b5344fb899bb053f82d2534355ce60aa3a
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <c17817@cray.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/36424
Reviewed-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexander Boyko <c17825@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
